name: AntiSandwichHook
description: A hook implementing sandwich-resistant AMM design that prevents MEV extraction by ensuring swaps execute at beginning-of-block prices. For zeroForOne swaps, the pool uses normal xy=k pricing. For non-zeroForOne swaps, the price is fixed at the beginning of the block, preventing price manipulation within a block. The hook inherits from BaseDynamicAfterFee and collects fees from price improvements.
whenToUse: Use when you want to protect users from sandwich attacks and front-running. This hook is particularly useful for DEXes that want to ensure fair pricing and prevent MEV extraction. Note: Only protects zeroForOne direction, and may result in prices that don't reflect current market conditions due to reduced arbitrage.
